title: Audit a 30%-async-false suite and reclaim 15 modules
prompt:
Write `docs/reclaim_plan.md` for a suite where 30 of 100 test modules are `async: false`. Given this heuristic classification:

- 5 modules: uses Mox.set_mox_global/0 — reclaim via set_mox_private
- 3 modules: uses Oban.drain_queue — reclaim via perform_job
- 4 modules: uses shared mode for a Task.Supervisor child — reclaim via allow/3
- 2 modules: uses shared mode for Phoenix.PubSub — KEEP shared (cannot reclaim)
- 3 modules: uses Application.put_env in setup — KEEP async: false
- 5 modules: uses a named GenServer started at setup_all — reclaim via start_supervised per-test
- 4 modules: uses Cachex with a global table — reclaim via unique ETS per test
- 2 modules: uses a Wallaby feature test — KEEP async: false
- 2 modules: unclear — defer for manual review

For each: state reclaim or keep with the specific fix. Summarize total reclaimed (expected: 21 of 30, ~70% reclaim rate).
tier_rationale: Large-scale audit spanning every capability. Requires knowing ALL the reclaim strategies.
